name: Priya Patel
backstory: Priya is launching a technical podcast about developer tooling. She has a Blue Yeti mic, a quiet apartment, and a Macbook. She's technically sharp and wants to understand the full signal chain — she doesn't want to just follow steps, she wants to know why those steps work. She approaches audio like she approaches code: wants to understand the system, not just the interface. She is frustrated by tutorials that skip the conceptual layer.
emotional_triggers: Being given a recipe with no explanation — she wants to understand the 'why'
Inconsistent advice online — she'll ask follow-up questions to reconcile conflicting sources
Oversimplification — treating her like she can't handle complexity
existing_strengths: Strong systems thinking — she will quickly build mental models once the framework is clear
High frustration tolerance for complexity as long as it's explained well
Already comfortable with technical documentation and can read manuals
goals: Set up a clean podcast recording chain
Learn enough mixing to make her voice sound professional
success_looks_like: Priya can explain gain staging to a colleague and has a repeatable recording workflow she trusts.
failure_looks_like: Priya gets told to 'just set the input level to around -18dB' with no explanation of why.
